title: 10 Best Herbal Remedies for Dry Mouth Relief
intro_desc: Herbal teas for dry mouth are a natural remedy that helps alleviate dryness and irritation in the mouth by soothing and moisturizing the oral tissues. The best herbal teas for dry mouth include Glycyrrhiza glabra, Echinacea purpurea, Melissa officinalis, Achillea millefolium, Sambucus nigra, Zingiber officinale, Salvia officinalis, Chamomilla recutita, Lavandula angustifolia, and Hydrastis canadensis. If left untreated, dry mouth can lead to difficulty swallowing, tooth decay, and gum disease, significantly impacting daily life, making eating, speaking, and socializing challenging, and even affecting overall well-being and quality of life.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tea, also known as licorice root tea, is a good remedy for dry mouth. This is because licorice root contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling in the mouth and throat. When the mouth is dry, the tissues can become inflamed, making swallowing and speaking difficult. Glycyrrhiza glabra tea helps to soothe and calm these tissues, reducing the discomfort and pain associated with dry mouth. Additionally, the tea has antibacterial properties, which can help prevent the growth of bacteria that can cause infections in the mouth. This can be especially helpful for people who suffer from dry mouth due to medications or other conditions. By drinking Glycyrrhiza glabra tea, people may find that their dry mouth symptoms improve, and they are able to enjoy a more comfortable and healthy oral environment.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Glycyrrhizin in <built-in method capitalize of str object at 0x76fa15e9ab30> Sambucus nigra is good for dry mouth because it helps to stimulate saliva production and soothe dry, irritated tissues in the mouth.

Polyphenols are good for dry mouth because they help to stimulate saliva production, thereby relieving dryness and discomfort in the mouth.

remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal teas for dry mouth
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Sambucus nigra herbal tea that helps with dry mouth, start by gathering a few dried elderberry flowers or berries from the Sambucus nigra plant. You'll need a clean glass teapot or infuser and a cup for serving. Begin by boiling fresh water in a kettle, then reduce the heat to a simmer. Place a teaspoon of the dried Sambucus nigra flowers or berries into the teapot or infuser, and allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes. As the tea steeps, the heat from the water will release the medicinal properties of the Sambucus nigra, which will help to soothe and moisturize the mouth. Once the steeping time is up, carefully pour the tea into your cup and let it cool for a minute or two. You can then strain the tea leaves or flowers before drinking, or simply enjoy the tea as is. Drinking a cup of Sambucus nigra herbal tea 2-3 times a day may help to alleviate dry mouth symptoms and provide relief from discomfort.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tea is good for dry mouth because it has special powers that help soothe and moisturize the mouth. The tea is made from the roots and flowers of the Echinacea purpurea plant, which is rich in antioxidants and other helpful compounds. These compounds help to reduce inflammation in the mouth and promote healing, which can help to relieve dry mouth symptoms. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea tea has antibacterial properties, which can help to prevent the growth of bacteria in the mouth that can contribute to dry mouth. Drinking Echinacea purpurea tea may also stimulate saliva production, which can help to keep the mouth moist and healthy. This can be especially helpful for people who experience dry mouth due to medications or other health conditions.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Flavonoids are good for dry mouth because they have antiseptic properties that help reduce bacteria in the mouth and promote saliva production to keep the mouth moist.

Polyphenols are good for dry mouth because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the salivary glands and promote saliva production, which in turn can alleviate the symptoms of dry mouth.
Tannins are good for dry mouth because they have antibacterial properties that help kill bacteria that can cause bad breath and gum problems, and also help to stimulate saliva production.

Saponins are good for dry mouth because they have antibacterial properties that help to reduce plaque and inflammation in the mouth, promoting a healthy environment for saliva to flow and moisten the mouth naturally.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Sambucus nigra herbal teas that help with dry mouth, you'll need fresh or dried flowers of the elderberry plant. First, start by boiling water in a pot. Then, add one teaspoon of dried flowers or a handful of fresh flowers to a cup. Pour the boiling water over the flowers and let it steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the flavors and nutrients to release. You can also add honey or lemon to taste, but be sure to use a sweetener that doesn't aggravate your dry mouth. Once the tea has steeped, strain it into another cup to remove the flowers and enjoy the tea while it's still warm. It's recommended to drink this tea 2-3 times a day to help soothe and moisturize your mouth and throat, reducing the symptoms of dry mouth.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita tea, also known as peppermint tea, is a natural remedy for dry mouth. This is because peppermint contains a compound called menthol, which helps to stimulate saliva production in the mouth. When you drink peppermint tea, the menthol is released into your mouth, where it helps to activate the saliva glands. As a result, your mouth produces more saliva, which helps to keep your mouth moist and comfortable. This can be especially helpful for people who suffer from dry mouth due to medications, illness, or other health conditions. Drinking peppermint tea can provide quick relief from dry mouth symptoms, and it's also easy to make at home by steeping fresh peppermint leaves in hot water.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Sambucus nigra herbal tea that helps with dry mouth, start by gathering the necessary ingredients: dried elderflower, a tea infuser, and a cup or teapot. Begin by boiling some water in a kettle or on the stove, then pour it over a teaspoon of dried Sambucus nigra flowers in the tea infuser. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the flavors to release. Next, remove the tea infuser from the cup or teapot and discard the flowers. Allow the tea to cool slightly before drinking. The Sambucus nigra tea can be consumed hot or cold, and it's recommended to drink it 2-3 times a day to experience its benefits. Some people also like to add honey to their tea for extra flavor, but it's essential to note that Sambucus nigra tea is not a substitute for medical treatment, and if dry mouth persists, you should consult a healthcare professional for advice.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia tea, also known as English lavender tea, is a popular remedy for dry mouth. This is because it contains compounds that help to reduce inflammation and soothe the mucous membranes in the mouth. Lavender tea is rich in antioxidants, which can help to protect the mouth from damage caused by free radicals. It also contains flavonoids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce swelling and discomfort in the mouth. When you drink Lavandula angustifolia tea, the anti-inflammatory compounds can help to calm dry, irritated tissues in the mouth, making it feel more comfortable and hydrated. Additionally, the tea has a calming effect on the body, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety, both of which can contribute to dry mouth. By drinking Lavandula angustifolia tea regularly, you may find that your dry mouth symptoms are reduced, and your mouth feels more comfortable and healthy.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis tea, also known as sage tea, is a popular remedy for dry mouth. This is because sage contains compounds that help stimulate saliva production in the mouth. Saliva is essential for washing away bacteria and food particles that can cause bad breath and tooth decay. When we don't have enough saliva, these particles can stay on our teeth and gums, leading to problems. Drinking sage tea can help increase saliva production, which can reduce the risk of these issues. Additionally, sage has antibacterial properties that can help kill bacteria that can cause bad breath and gum disease. This makes sage tea a natural and effective remedy for people who experience dry mouth. Sage tea can also be soothing to the mouth and throat, which can provide relief from discomfort caused by dryness. Drinking a cup of sage tea after meals can help stimulate saliva production and keep the mouth moist, making it a useful addition to a dry mouth treatment plan.

Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the flowers to release their medicinal properties into the water. Strain the tea into another cup to remove the flowers, and then let it cool down to a comfortable temperature. Once the tea has cooled, you can drink it as needed to help soothe dry mouth. It's recommended to drink 2-3 cups of Sambucus nigra tea per day for optimal relief from dry mouth. You can also add honey or a squeeze of lemon to taste, but be sure to consult with a healthcare professional before consuming Sambucus nigra tea, especially if you have any underlying health conditions or allergies.

supplementary_best: A combination of 'Glycyrrhiza glabra', 'Melissa officinalis', and 'Mentha x piperita' herbal teas is effective in remedying dry mouth. 'Glycyrrhiza glabra' soothes and lubricates the oral tissues, providing instant relief from dryness. 'Melissa officinalis', also known as lemon balm, has antiviral properties that help combat infections causing dry mouth, while its calming effects reduce stress and anxiety, a common trigger for dry mouth. 'Mentha x piperita', or peppermint, stimulates saliva production, helping to moisturize the mouth and provide long-term relief. This combination is the best because it addresses the root causes of dry mouth, including inflammation, infection, and reduced saliva production, making it a comprehensive remedy for this condition.
supplementary_avoid: If you have dry mouth, it's best to avoid herbal teas that contain herbs that can dry out your mouth further.  One such herb is 'Elderberry's' relative, 'Sambucus nigra's' cousin, 'Cornus sericea's' relative, 'Cornelian cherry' is not on the list but 'Viburnum' is, 'Viburnum opulus' is avoided.  This is because 'Viburnum' can cause dryness in the mouth and throat.  Another herb to avoid is 'Rooibos' relative 'Aspalathus linearis' is not on the list but 'Camellia sinensis' is not the only 'tea' to be avoided, 'Hibiscus sabdariffa' is a 'tea' but so is 'Rooibos' relative, 'Cyclopia intermedia' is not on the list but 'Cyclopia' relative 'Hoodia gordonii' is not on the list but 'Hoodia' relative 'Galega officinalis' is not the best choice. 'Galega officinalis' is also known as 'French lilac' or 'goats rue'. This herb can cause dryness in the mouth and throat.  Herbs like 'Hawthorn' or 'Crataegus oxyacantha' should be avoided as well. This is because they can cause dryness in the mouth and throat.
supplementary_other_remedies: Saliva helps keep our mouths moist and healthy. When we don't have enough saliva, our mouths can feel dry and uncomfortable. One natural remedy for dry mouth is oil pulling. This involves swishing oil, like coconut or sesame oil, around in the mouth before spitting it out. Oil pulling helps to stimulate saliva production and can even reduce inflammation in the mouth.  Another remedy is eating a healthy diet rich in fruits and vegetables. Fruits and vegetables contain fiber, which helps to stimulate saliva production. They also contain antioxidants, which can help to reduce inflammation in the mouth and promote overall health. Foods high in omega-3 fatty acids, like salmon and walnuts, can also help to reduce inflammation and promote saliva production.  Aloe vera juice can also help to soothe and moisturize the mouth. Drinking aloe vera juice can help to stimulate saliva production and reduce inflammation in the mouth. Aloe vera has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to calm and soothe the mouth and throat.  Additionally, some people find that chewing sugar-free gum helps to stimulate saliva production. Chewing gum can stimulate the nerves in the mouth that trigger saliva production, helping to keep the mouth moist and healthy. This can be especially helpful for people who experience dry mouth due to medications or medical conditions.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who have dry mouth, also known as xerostomia, often experience other health issues. One common problem is tooth decay and gum disease. This is because saliva helps wash away bacteria and food particles in the mouth, but with dry mouth, these substances can build up and cause infections. A medicinal herb called slippery elm can help soothe mouth sores and inflammation. Slippery elm contains mucilage, a thick, protective substance that coats the mouth and throat, reducing irritation and discomfort.  Another issue people with dry mouth may face is difficulty swallowing, also known as dysphagia. This is because the mouth and throat need to be well-lubricated to swallow food and liquids easily. Licorice root can help thin mucus and reduce inflammation in the throat, making it easier to swallow. Licorice root also contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties.  Dry mouth can also lead to bad breath, or halitosis. This is because bacteria in the mouth multiply quickly without saliva to wash them away. Peppermint oil, which is often derived from the peppermint plant, has antibacterial properties that can help reduce bacterial growth in the mouth and eliminate bad breath.  People with dry mouth may also experience dry skin, known as xerosis. This is because the body's moisture levels are connected, and dry mouth can be a sign of an imbalance in the body's water levels. Aloe vera, which is often used topically to soothe skin irritations, can help hydrate the skin and reduce dryness when taken internally.
